beginner.question: What are design tokens in CSS?
beginner.answer: Design tokens are the atomic design decisions (colors, spacing, typography, shadows) stored as named values that form the foundation of a design system.

beginner.question: What is a CSS design system?
beginner.answer: A CSS design system is a collection of reusable components, design tokens, and guidelines that ensure visual and functional consistency across a product.

beginner.question: How are CSS custom properties used in design systems?
beginner.answer: CSS custom properties store design tokens (--color-primary, --spacing-md) for consistent, themeable, and maintainable component styling.

beginner.question: What is a component library in CSS?
beginner.answer: A component library is a collection of pre-built, reusable UI components (buttons, cards, modals) styled with a consistent design language.

beginner.question: How do design tokens improve consistency?
beginner.answer: Tokens centralize design decisions into reusable variables, ensuring every component uses the same colors, spacing, and typography throughout.

beginner.question: What is the difference between a design system and a style guide?
beginner.answer: A design system includes code, components, and patterns; a style guide documents visual standards and usage guidelines.

beginner.question: How do CSS custom properties enable theming?
beginner.answer: Custom properties can be redefined at different scopes (e.g., [data-theme='dark']) to apply different values for light and dark themes.

beginner.question: What types of values are stored as design tokens?
beginner.answer: Colors, typography (font family, sizes, weights), spacing (margin, padding), shadows, border-radius, breakpoints, z-index values, and animation durations.

beginner.question: How do you organize CSS custom properties in a design system?
beginner.answer: Group by category: --color-*, --spacing-*, --font-*, --shadow-*, --radius-*, --animation-* for clear naming and discoverability.

beginner.question: What is the relationship between design tokens and component libraries?
beginner.answer: Component libraries consume design tokens as their source of truth; tokens define the visual language that components implement.

intermediate.question: How do you create a scalable naming convention for design tokens?
How do you implement dark mode with CSS custom properties?
intermediate.answer: Define tokens on :root for light mode, then override with [data-theme='dark'] selector: --color-bg: #000; components use var(--color-bg) automatically.

intermediate.question: How do you version design tokens in a design system?
intermediate.answer: Use semantic versioning for token packages, maintain a changelog, and provide migration guides when token values change.

intermediate.question: How do design tokens integrate with preprocessors like Sass?
intermediate.answer: Define tokens as SCSS variables, then pass them to CSS custom properties: $color-primary: #007bff; --color-primary: #{$color-primary}; for both runtime and compile-time use.

intermediate.question: How do you structure CSS custom properties for responsive design tokens?
intermediate.answer: Use media queries to redefine tokens: @media (min-width: 768px) { :root { --spacing-md: 24px; } } values update automatically in all consumers.

intermediate.question: What is the role of the :root selector in design systems?
intermediate.answer: :root is the global scope where base design tokens are defined; all components can access these tokens via var() anywhere in the document.

intermediate.question: How do you document design tokens for developers?
intermediate.answer: Use tools like Style Dictionary to generate token documentation, or maintain a CSS file with commented token groups and visual examples.

intermediate.question: How do component libraries handle CSS specificity with design tokens?
intermediate.answer: Components use class-based selectors with low specificity and reference tokens via var(); this keeps components easy to override and theme.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between global tokens and component tokens?
intermediate.answer: Global tokens (--color-primary) are shared across all components; component tokens (--btn-bg) are scoped to specific components and reference global tokens.

intermediate.question: How do you ensure backward compatibility when changing design tokens?
intermediate.answer: Deprecate tokens gradually by marking them as @deprecated, providing replacement tokens, and running codemods to update existing usage.

advanced.question: How do you implement a multi-brand design system with CSS custom properties?
advanced.answer: Define brand-specific token sets in separate selectors: [data-brand='acme'] { --color-primary: #...; } [data-brand='globex'] { --color-primary: #...; }

advanced.question: How do you use Style Dictionary to generate platform-specific tokens?
advanced.answer: Style Dictionary reads JSON token files and outputs platform formats: CSS custom properties for web, JSON for iOS, XML for Android.

advanced.question: How do you handle token aliasing (aliasing one token to another)?
advanced.answer: Create alias tokens that reference base tokens: --color-link: var(--color-primary); --color-link-hover: var(--color-primary-dark); for semantic mapping.

advanced.question: How do you implement high-contrast mode tokens for accessibility?
advanced.answer: Use @media (prefers-contrast: high) { :root { --color-text: #000; --color-bg: #fff; } } to override tokens for high-contrast preferences.

advanced.question: How do you optimize design system CSS bundle size?
advanced.answer: Tree-shake unused tokens, use utility classes, purge unused component CSS, and split critical CSS for initial load.

advanced.question: How do you manage breaking changes in a distributed design system?
advanced.answer: Use major version bumps for breaking token changes, provide migration scripts, and maintain a codemod for automated upgrades.

advanced.question: How do design tokens work with runtime CSS-in-JS libraries?
advanced.answer: CSS-in-JS can import token objects and inject them as CSS custom properties or use them directly in styled components via JavaScript variables.

advanced.question: How do you test design tokens for consistency and accessibility?
advanced.answer: Write automated tests that verify color contrast ratios, spacing increments follow a scale, and typography hierarchy is maintained.

advanced.question: How do you create a token debugging overlay for development?
advanced.answer: Build a debug panel that displays current token values from computed styles, allowing developers to inspect active token values in real-time.

advanced.question: How do you handle responsive tokens in a design system without media queries in components?
advanced.answer: Define responsive tokens in media queries at the root level; components remain media-query-free and simply use var() tokens that respond automatically.
